behaviors.description: All direct and transitive dependencies are extracted from the manifest and included in the vulnerability check.
behaviors.justification: Failure is most likely at module handoff boundaries (manifest parser -> dependency graph/transitive resolver -> vulnerability-check input builder), not a single-function algorithm. No explicit need for real network/OS/subprocess, no AI dependency, no multi-agent chain. Higher level rejected: System is disqualified because behavior text does not require real external contract validation or external side-effect readback.
behaviors.test_description: Run scanner pipeline in-process against fixture manifests/lockfiles containing direct and transitive deps; assert the emitted vulnerability-query input contains the complete expected dependency set (name + resolved version/range) across modules.

behaviors.description: The vulnerability database query returns results that match the exact version ranges in the manifest, not just the package name.
behaviors.justification: This behavior depends on real external API query semantics for version ranges. A mock can hide mismatches in how the real service interprets range syntax or filters CVEs. SYSTEM TRIAD trigger applies: verify real external happy-path response/contract for range-specific matches. Higher level rejected: Agentic/Workflow disqualified because no AI/LLM decisions are involved.
behaviors.test_description: Against a real vulnerability DB test endpoint/dataset, submit queries built from manifest ranges (including edge ranges) and assert returned CVEs correspond to the exact requested versions/ranges, not package-only matches; include round-trip response validation.

behaviors.description: The severity sorting places Critical above High above Medium above Low, with CVEs of the same severity sorted by CVSS score.
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic ordering logic in one boundary (comparator/sort function). Higher-level checks add little new reality signal if unit tests already cover tie-breaking and enum mapping. Higher level rejected: Integration/System disqualified because behavior text does not require cross-module infra interactions or external contracts.
behaviors.test_description: Feed unsorted CVE fixtures with mixed severities and CVSS ties into the sort function and assert exact ordered output: severity precedence first, CVSS descending within same severity.

behaviors.description: The manifest modification changes only the affected dependency's version, leaving all other entries unchanged.
behaviors.justification: Risk is in parser->mutator->serializer/file-update interactions (preserving unrelated entries) rather than just semver math. Local filesystem/module wiring is enough; no explicit real external API contract required. Higher level rejected: System is disqualified because behavior text does not require external service contract checks or external side-effect visibility beyond local project files.
behaviors.test_description: Execute manifest update flow on real fixture files (e.g., package.json/requirements.txt) and assert file diff modifies exactly one dependency version while all other lines/entries remain unchanged.

behaviors.description: The auto-generated pull request is created on the real repository with the correct single-dependency diff.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires real repository side effect and correctness of externally visible PR diff. SYSTEM TRIAD trigger applies (round-trip side-effect visibility + external contract). Mocks can falsely pass while real VCS/API behavior fails.
behaviors.test_description: Run scanner against an integration test repo and verify via real Git hosting API/UI readback that a PR was created, branch exists, and the PR diff contains exactly one dependency-version change.

behaviors.description: A major version bump is correctly identified as a breaking change based on semver comparison.
behaviors.justification: Semver major-bump detection is deterministic local logic and can be validated at a single function/object boundary. Higher level rejected: Integration/System disqualified because no external infra or cross-module handoff is required by this behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Unit-test semver comparison with representative cases (major/minor/patch, pre-release/build metadata, range operators) and assert only major bump cases are flagged breaking.

behaviors.description: When a breaking change is detected, PR creation is skipped and a review flag is surfaced in the scanner output.
behaviors.justification: This is orchestration/state handoff across components: breaking-change detector -> PR orchestration gate -> output/reporting. Not a single-function check. No explicit requirement to validate real external PR API contract; can be proven in-process by asserting no PR action and correct output flag. Higher level rejected: System is disqualified because behavior text does not require real external readback (only skip action + surfaced local scanner output).
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end in-process scan with a fixture that triggers breaking change; assert PR creation path is not invoked and scanner output/report contains the expected review-required flag/state.
